llm_summary.one_line: Matthew needs APN number, cutover date, and confirmation on Wufoo paperwork completion.
llm_summary.summary: Team has consensus to switch US AWS customers from Ingram to TD Synnex. Adam looped in Lacie, Erik, and Andrei for customer migration planning and will book a coordination call. Matthew is now asking Adam for the US region APN number, proposed cutover date (suggesting 3/22), and whether Adam completed required Wufoo paperwork. April 1 deadline may be missed with financial impact on Basis.
my_actions: Provide US region APN number to Matthew
Confirm or propose cutover date (Matthew suggested 3/22)
Confirm whether Wufoo paperwork was completed
Book coordination call with Lacie, Erik, Andrei, Kristina, and Matthew
tracked_actions: Matthew will initiate supplier transition once he receives APN and date
Lacie's team (possibly Kristina) will lead per-customer migration documentation
Andrei will manage customer impact (billing migration, CloudCheckr access)
Erik will handle billing coordination at month-end
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Hi Matt, I need to get the APN number from Marc—I'll follow up with him and get that to you today. For the cutover date, let's discuss on the coordination call I'm booking since we need to align with Lacie, Erik, and Andrei on customer migration timing. I haven't completed the Wufoo paperwork yet—can you send me the link? I'll knock it out this afternoon.

llm_summary.one_line: Digital Bridge info request due EOD tomorrow; Adam must provide contract expiry data for top 10 clients.
llm_summary.summary: Sarah requested updated information for Digital Bridge with EOD tomorrow deadline. Multiple team members tagged for different data points. Adam was specifically tagged (along with Kathy Wu or Tom Kelsch) to provide contract expiry information for top 10 clients. Dennis and Tom have already responded with their portions (contribution margin approach and sales pipeline data respectively). Adam has not yet responded regarding contract expiry data.
my_actions: Provide contract expiry dates for top 10 clients by EOD tomorrow, or indicate alternate timing
Coordinate with Kathy Wu or Tom Kelsch if needed to compile contract expiry information
tracked_actions: Paul Billard providing MRR, LOB scope, and customer demographics
Dennis Lam and Paul Billard collaborating on contribution margin data
Tom Kelsch already delivered sales pipeline data
Kathy Wu providing booked backlog data
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Hi Sarah, I'll work with Kathy and Tom to pull together the contract expiry data for the top 10 clients. We should be able to get this to you by EOD tomorrow as requested. Will coordinate with them and send over the information once compiled.

threads.subject: 2026 Annual Security And Compliance Vendor Review - Pagerduty

llm_summary.one_line: Melvin following up on PagerDuty vendor security review—needs Adam's comments/approval.
llm_summary.summary: Melvin Ching is following up on the 2026 annual security and compliance vendor review for PagerDuty, originally sent February 20. He prepared a report based on vendor compliance documentation (SOC 2, ISO 27001, etc.) and needs Adam to review and provide comments via Appendix B or email reply. Melvin also encouraged a full business review with Nedim Deovic and offered Fred Taylor-Young for security-specific discussions.
my_actions: Review the PagerDuty vendor security report prepared by Melvin
Provide comments/approval on the report via Appendix B or email reply
Consider whether to conduct full annual business review (financials, SLAs) and engage Nedim if proceeding
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Hi Melvin, apologies for the delay. I'll review the PagerDuty report and get you my comments by end of week. Will also evaluate whether we need the full business review with Nedim. Thanks for the thorough prep work on this.

threads.subject: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - Sql Server License Verification

llm_summary.one_line: SQL Server license compliance issue; Fred says Abuse shouldn't police customer licensing violations.
llm_summary.summary: A customer appears to be violating SQL Server licensing terms. Barbara believes Aptum could be liable if audited by Microsoft. Adam looped in Fred Taylor-Young from InfoSec/Abuse, but Fred clarifies that Abuse handles service misuse (bitcoin mining, illegal sites), not license compliance. Fred questions whether Aptum should police customer software licensing at all, noting potential liability if misinterpreted. Adam needs to decide how to handle this compliance risk and who owns it.
my_actions: Determine internal ownership for customer software license compliance issues
Decide whether to enforce SQL Server licensing with this customer or escalate to legal/commercial teams
Clarify policy on Aptum's liability exposure for customer license violations
tracked_actions: Fred Taylor-Young documenting Abuse team scope after previous manager departure
Ticket 12028 resolution pending direction on license enforcement
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Thanks Fred. You raise a valid point about scope and liability. Barbara, let's escalate this to our legal/commercial team to clarify our contractual obligations around customer licensing compliance. In parallel, can someone brief me on the specific Microsoft license violation and our exposure? We need a clear policy on whether we enforce third-party software licensing or if that responsibility sits with the customer per our terms of service.

threads.subject: Eboards Margin Recovery On Future Orders

llm_summary.one_line: Matthew reminding team to recover $2K/mo margin on eBoards renewals; Roger acknowledged.
llm_summary.summary: Matthew Carter is reminding the account team that eBoards Imperva contracts are running at 8% margin (vs 35% target) and losing money. He's instructing that any future eBoards renewals or upgrades must include an additional $2K/mo to offset these losses. Roger questioned the margin issue but Matthew redirected focus to solving it going forward. Roger has acknowledged and committed to keeping this in mind for renewals.
tracked_actions: Roger Gonzales to incorporate $2K/mo margin recovery into upcoming eBoards renewals (Sep '26, Nov '26, Oct '27)

llm_summary.one_line: TD SYNNEX confirms partnership for AWS CTA transfer, US onboarding by March 22.
llm_summary.summary: TD SYNNEX (Christine Thomson) confirms Aptum's decision to move forward with them for AWS services. US team (Ciara Perkins, Deb Neumeier) will handle US onboarding with CTA requested for March 22. Canadian team (Christine, Rupesh, Maz) will continue supporting Canadian region and will activate Aptum's Canadian account for future business.
tracked_actions: US team (Ciara/Deb) onboarding Aptum for AWS CTA by March 22
Rupesh sending background information to US team
Canadian account activation in progress

threads.subject: Request For Soc 2 Type Ii Report And Iso 27001 Certificate - Vendor Compliance Documentation

llm_summary.one_line: Customer compliance request escalated; GSE-306 opened, Steve meeting Andy tomorrow morning.
llm_summary.summary: Frederic forwarded a customer compliance documentation request (SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001) that the team initially missed. Matthew Carter followed up with customer Charles. A GSE (GSE-306) has been opened to track this. Steve Rioux will discuss with Andy Petterson tomorrow morning to address the situation.
tracked_actions: Steve Rioux meeting with Andy Petterson tomorrow morning regarding customer compliance request
GSE-306 tracking the SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 documentation request

threads.subject: Placeholder Event: Preview Of The New Program Through Td Synnex Along With All The New Incentives

llm_summary.one_line: TD Synnex transition for AWS DSA/CTA sponsorship; March 22 move date requested.
llm_summary.summary: Matthew Carter notified TD Synnex that Aptum is moving forward with changing DSA/CTA sponsorship to TD Synnex in the US region, with a requested transition date of March 22. This follows a meeting where TD Synnex presented upcoming AWS partner program changes effective April 1st, including new discount structures and deprecated legacy incentives. Matthew has already contacted Amazon's distribution program team to initiate the move.
tracked_actions: Matthew Carter coordinating DSA/CTA sponsorship transition to TD Synnex with March 22 target date
TD Synnex to provide required documentation/information for transition completion

llm_summary.one_line: Paul confirms Lacie's churn report with service IDs and ticket links fills reporting gap.
llm_summary.summary: Paul Billard requested churn analysis for large February 2026 losses. Lacie provided updated comments in a shared sheet, noting some customers lack Ocean ticket documentation. She also shared BI Report (BI-53) for Paul's review as a potential solution. Paul confirmed the churn report with service IDs and ticket hyperlinks should fill the gap. This appears resolved between Paul and Lacie.
tracked_actions: Paul reviewing BI Report (BI-53) that Lacie shared
Ongoing discussions to improve cancellation categories in Ocean system

threads.subject: 💚 Vos Équipes Sont Épuisées? Des Pistes Concrètes Pour Agir

llm_summary.one_line: Marketing email for French-language mental health and well-being conference on April 16.
llm_summary.summary: Promotional email from Événements Les Affaires advertising a conference on workplace mental health, well-being, and managing team burnout scheduled for April 16. The event focuses on preventing psychosocial risks, supporting team energy, and managing hyperconnectivity. This is external marketing content in French, not directly related to Aptum/CloudOps operations.

llm_summary.one_line: Microsoft statement of account for account 003013508832 with payment reminder.
llm_summary.summary: Automated statement of account from Microsoft Treasury for account 003013508832. Standard reminder to review details, settle open invoices by due date, and utilize any credits. No specific issues or amounts mentioned in the email body.
tracked_actions: Review attached Microsoft statement for account 003013508832 for accuracy and any open balances

llm_summary.one_line: When I Work offering account check-in with scheduling expert for optimization tips.
llm_summary.summary: When I Work (scheduling software) is offering Adam an account check-in session with a scheduling expert. The session would cover account optimization, best practices to reduce manual work, and new features. This is a standard customer success outreach from the vendor.

threads.subject: Distressed Condo Projects Likely To Become Seniors Residences

llm_summary.one_line: Commercial real estate newsletter covering Canadian property development and market trends.
llm_summary.summary: Industry newsletter from Connect CRE News covering various Canadian commercial real estate developments. Headline story discusses distressed condo projects potentially converting to seniors residences. Other items include CPKC land development, retail openings, and residential projects across Canada. This is informational content with no action required.

threads.subject: Last Chance! Ai Security: Ai Agents In Servicenow

llm_summary.one_line: Marketing webinar invitation for AI security in ServiceNow on March 17.
llm_summary.summary: BeyondTrust is promoting a webinar about AI agents in ServiceNow and their security implications, scheduled for March 17 at 1:00pm EDT. This is a standard marketing email from an external vendor. No action is required unless Adam has specific interest in ServiceNow AI security topics.

threads.subject: Breaking News: Public Storage To Acquire National Storage Affiliates In All-Stock $10.5B Deal

llm_summary.one_line: Public Storage acquiring National Storage Affiliates for $10.5B in all-stock deal.
llm_summary.summary: Connect CRE News reports that Public Storage is acquiring National Storage Affiliates Trust (NSA) in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $10.5 billion enterprise value. This is a major consolidation move in the self-storage industry. No action required from Adam; this is industry news for awareness.

threads.subject: Request For Earlier Cloud Billing Visibility

llm_summary.one_line: Azure invoices now released mid-month (15th); MO365 remains end-of-month. New GP batch naming.
llm_summary.summary: Brad Wong confirms Azure invoices are now released on the 15th of each month instead of month-end, improving billing visibility. Azure GP batches will use format AZUREMMYYYY to differentiate from MO365 services. MO365 invoices remain on their existing end-of-month schedule. This change was coordinated with Tim and Andy as previously discussed.
tracked_actions: Jorge Quintero implementing new Azure GP batch naming convention (AZUREMMYYYY format)
